title: terminalia longespicata subsp. longespicata
intro: Terminalia longespicata subsp. longespicata is a sub-species of the Terminalia longespicata tree, native to the tropical forests of the Western Ghats in India. This tree is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, malaria, and digestive issues. The medicinal properties of Terminalia longespicata subsp. longespicata are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant can be prepared into various herbal preparations, such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, skin problems, and wounds.
